title: Vanilla Custard Slice
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Donna Hay-style custard slice: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
intro: This is my independent Thermomix homage to Donna Hay and the comforting appeal of custard slice. It keeps the spirit of the original inspiration, but the method is written fresh for TM6 and TM7 cooks who want clear settings and a warm, achievable result.
ingredients: 250 g key fruit, chocolate or dairy ingredient as needed
2 eggs or 300 g cream, depending on dish
120 g plain flour or almond meal
steps: Add sugar and any citrus zest or dry flavouring to the mixing bowl and mill 10 sec / speed 9.
Add dairy, eggs or fruit base as suited to the dessert and cook 7 min / 90°C / speed 3 until smooth and thickened.
Scrape down, then blend for a silky texture 15 sec / speed 6.
Pour into dishes or a tin and chill or bake as needed; finish 2 hr before serving
tips: Read the full method before starting so the Thermomix settings flow smoothly.
Keep the measuring cup off when reducing or sautéing so steam can escape.
Taste at the end and adjust salt, lemon or herbs to suit your ingredients.

faq.q: Is this the original Donna Hay recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Donna Hay and custard slice; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Vanilla Custard Slice in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.
Yes. Most components can be prepared earlier the same day, then reheated or finished just before serving for the best texture.
